observations: Well-structured implementation with TurboQuantKVCacheManager for KV cache compression
Proper abstraction with TurboQuantAttentionWrapper for intercepting attention calls
Clean handling of vLLM availability with stub implementation fallback
Comprehensive health check method providing detailed status information
Unit tests cover backend initialization, health checks, generate, and chat methods
Mock-based tests handle the case where vLLM is not installed (Python 3.14+)
Tests verify error handling for uninitialized backend
Streaming and non-streaming generation paths are tested
Proper separation between VLLMBackend and VLLMBackendStub
TurboQuant integration follows the paper's approach with QJL projection
Monkey-patching strategy documented for vLLM integration

nonBlocking.description: vLLM monkey-patching implementation is incomplete - the turboquant_forward method stores original result but doesn't actually use TurboQuant compression in the attention computation
nonBlocking.suggestion: Complete the integration by properly decompressing keys and using compute_attention_with_compressed_keys

nonBlocking.description: Compression ratio calculation in compress_layer_cache may not be accurate for all layer shapes
nonBlocking.suggestion: Validate compression ratio calculation with actual vLLM models

sharedStateObservations.observation: vLLM backend handles Python 3.14+ gracefully by providing a stub implementation that raises informative errors. Pattern: check VLLM_AVAILABLE constant before importing vLLM-specific classes.
TurboQuant KV cache compression uses QJL (Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss) projection with Lloyd-Max quantization. Key compression is 3-bit, value compression is 2-bit.
vLLM integration requires monkey-patching attention layers. The model structure is accessed via llm.llm_engine.model_executor.driver_worker.model_runner.model.layers[i].self_attn
